compiled_state.title: As a developer, I get expanded test coverage when I change a highly-imported file
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add centrality scoring to record-test-status.sh using ast-grep. For each staged source file, compute how many other project files directly import/reference it. When the score exceeds test_gate.centrality_threshold (default: 8), run the full test suite instead of only associated tests. Gracefully degrade when ast-grep (sg) is not installed.

**Why**: Cross-cutting bugs where a change to a central file breaks dependent scripts are missed by the current fuzzy-matching test gate. Centrality awareness expands coverage proportionally to how many files depend on the changed file.

**Scope**:
- IN: Core centrality computation in record-test-status.sh, threshold comparison logic, full test suite triggering via commands.test config key, graceful degradation when sg not installed (defaults to 0 + stderr warning)
- OUT: Per-language config patterns (separate story), caching (separate story), JSONL logging (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, record-test-status.sh computes a centrality score (direct reference count via ast-grep) for each staged source file
  ← Satisfies: "record-test-status.sh computes a centrality score for each staged source file"
- When this story is complete, any staged file with centrality exceeding test_gate.centrality_threshold (default: 8) triggers the full test suite (commands.test) instead of only associated tests
  ← Satisfies: "When any staged file's centrality score exceeds threshold, run full test suite"
- When this story is complete, running on a system without ast-grep (sg) produces a one-line stderr warning, defaults centrality to 0, and behaves identically to current behavior
  ← Satisfies: "Graceful degradation when sg not installed"
- When the full test suite runs (centrality threshold exceeded), the test-gate-status file records the outcome in the same format (passed/failed/timeout, diff_hash, timestamp, tested_files) that pre-commit-test-gate.sh expects — ensuring the pre-commit hook continues to validate correctly
  ← Satisfies: "The pre-commit test gate hook is unchanged"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] record-test-status.sh has SIGURG/timeout handling (exit 144, resume support) — centrality computation must not break this contract
- [Reliability] Graceful degradation when sg not installed must be tested on systems without ast-grep
- [Performance] ast-grep invocation per staged file adds wall-clock time; first-run could be 10-20s for 20 files
- [Integration] Verified: ast-grep supports sg run -p pattern -l lang --json for structural search
- [Integration] Risk: Bash source command AST parsing may differ — pattern needs validation against tree-sitter Bash grammar
- [Integration] record-test-status.sh currently has no mechanism to read or execute commands.test from dso-config.conf — S1 must add config-reading logic to resolve commands.test and invoke it as the full suite runner; define fallback behavior when commands.test is absent
- [Reliability] The current SIGURG/resume mechanism tracks individual test files. When centrality triggers a full suite run (a single commands.test invocation), the progress file has only one entry. If killed by SIGURG at 73s, the next resume re-runs the entire suite. Decide whether full-suite runs need a different resume strategy or if re-running is acceptable.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Centrality-aware test gate — record-test-status.sh computes fan-in scores, triggers full suite when threshold exceeded, graceful degradation, 14 tests
comments.author: Test
